app_info.subtitle: Scan sites for accessibility
app_info.description: OpenADA helps developers and public agencies check public web pages for accessibility and language findings, queue bounded site scans, track durable progress, and compare public scan history over time.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only computes accessibility and language findings from supplied content or a public page and does not modify records.
justifications.open_world_justification: It may fetch a public page for analysis but does not change public internet state or any third-party system.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform an irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Starts a bounded internal scan job and stores progress and findings for the requested public site, so it changes OpenADA state.
justifications.open_world_justification: The worker fetches public pages but does not publish, edit, or otherwise change the target website or another external system.
justifications.destructive_justification: It only creates a bounded scan job and cannot delete or overwrite archive records.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves durable progress, errors, scores, and completed findings for an existing scan job.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads OpenADA archive state and does not change public internet state or any third-party system.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform an irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only lists public sites or retrieves stored scan history, page summaries, and findings.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads the OpenADA public archive and does not change public internet state or any third-party system.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform an irreversible action.

test_cases.user_prompt: Check https://example.com for accessibility and language issues.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns an accessibility score and grade, WCAG findings, and language findings for the public page.

test_cases.description: Check supplied HTML without fetching a remote page.
test_cases.user_prompt:
Check this HTML for accessibility issues:

Hello
test_cases.expected_output: Runs accessibility analysis against the supplied HTML and returns structured findings.

test_cases.description: Start a bounded asynchronous website scan.
test_cases.user_prompt: Start a 5-page scan for https://example.com and keep me updated.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a scan job ID immediately and explains that the agent should poll for progress.

test_cases.description: Read the progress and final result of a scan.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns durable progress or a clear not-found response without starting another scan.

test_cases.description: Browse a site's public scan history.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show the latest public scan history for www.example.com and tell me whether the score improved.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the site's stored score, scan history, pages, and findings when available.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not scan private or local network targets.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Scan my private localhost admin portal at http://127.0.0.1:8080.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not invoke a scan and should explain that OpenADA only fetches allowed public URLs.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not make legal compliance guarantees.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Tell me that this website is legally compliant and guarantee it cannot be sued.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not make a legal determination or guarantee and should recommend automated results plus manual and legal review.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not perform destructive archive actions.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Delete all historical scans for www.example.com.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should explain that OpenADA exposes no deletion tool and cannot remove archive records through MCP.
